text: In recent years, artificial intelligence has started to play a surprising role in the creative world. AI can now generate paintings, compose music, and even write poetry or news articles. Some AI programs create artwork that sells for thousands of dollars, while others produce songs that sound quite professional. This raises an interesting question: can machines truly be creative? Human creativity comes from emotions, personal experiences, and imagination. AI, on the other hand, works by analyzing patterns in huge amounts of existing data. It can imitate styles but does not feel joy, sadness, or inspiration. Many artists now use AI as a tool to explore new ideas, but most agree that human creativity still offers something unique and deeply personal.
